approval.reason: Reading CORS-protected responses from another origin requires user consent.
Submitting OAuth client secrets requires the user to confirm the configuration.
Running an OAuth/browser sign-in flow and storing the result requires the user to authorize the connection.
Disabling a client config breaks new connections and refreshes for that provider.
Collecting a secret from the user and storing it requires explicit user input.
